english: and ṅed (nged) are used promiscuously in the same sentence, so: ṅas (ngas) I, and directly after: ṅed-kyi (nged kyi) our Mil. The plural number is specially indicated in: ṅed-cag (nged cag), ṅed-tso (nged tsho), ṅed-rnams (nged rnams), ṅed-dag (nged dag) Mil.; ṅed-čag-rnams (nged cag rnams) Cs. — ṅed-raṅ (nged rang) 1. I myself, we ourselves. 2. I, we Glr.; ṅed-ñid (nged nyid), ṅed-kó-na (nged kho na) Cs. id. (Ld. *ṅad* (ngad)).
Dzl. 202, 11. 15. is prob. an incor. reading in Sch.'s edition, instead of ṅu-bu-čag (ngu bu cag).

1. adj. certain, true, sure, firm, bdag-la ṅés-pa źig stsál-du gsol (bdag la nges pa zhig stsal du gsol) I ask you to communicate to me something certain, i.e. authentic news; ṅés-par byéd-pa (nges par byed pa) to fix, settle, establish, ascertain, e.g. facts of chronology, v. Wdk. chronological table in Cs.'s Grammar; to ratify Schr.; ₒči-bar ṅés-pa yin ('chi bar nges pa yin) or ṅés-so (nges so) death is certain; de bdén-par ṅés-sam (de bden par nges sam) is it certain that this is true? Glr.; mi btúb-tu ṅés-na (mi btub tu nges na) as it is certain that I am not able (to do it) Dzl.; nam ₒkyer ṅés-pa med (nam 'khyer nges pa med) it is not certain at what time they will be carried off Glr.; bdag kyód-kyi bu yin ṅés-na (bdag khyod kyi bu yin nges na) if I am actually, for certain, your son Pth.; pań ṅés-pai čos (phan nges pa'i chos) that religion which is sure to lead to salvation Mil.; ṅés-pai dón-las gol (nges pa'i don las gol) he is missing the true sense Pth.; ma-ṅés-pa (ma nges pa) untrue Tar. 109, 17; gnás-la ṅés-pa méd-pa yin (gnas la nges pa med pa yin) as to abode I am changeable, I have no fixed abode Mil.; also ṅes-méd (nges med) alone: homeless Mil.; undefined, ṅes-méd-kyi rí-la (nges med kyi ri la) somewhere on the mountains Mil.; sometimes it is but a rhetorical turn, like the English evidently, obviously, bú-la bkra-mi-śis ṅés-kyis (bu la bkra mi shis nges kyis), as our son has evidently met with an accident Dzl.; bud-méd yin-du ṅés-so (bud med yin du nges so) they are evidently women, they do not deserve to be called men Dzl.; also sbst. certainty, surety, truth; tsé-la, ṅág-la, lús-la ṅés-pa med (tshe la, ngag la, lus la nges pa med) (man's) life-time, word, body have no certainty, are transient Glr. Hence ṅés-pa-čan (nges pa can) real, actual, ṅés-pa-čan-du (nges pa can du) really, truly, in fact, in reality, opp. to deceitful appearances, false opinions, wrong calculations etc. Glr.; ṅes-pa-nyid-du (nges pa nyid du) adv. 1. in reality Glr. 2. truly, in truth, verily Glr.; ṅés-par (nges par) adv. 1. really, certainly, to be sure, frq.; sdig-pa byás-na rnam-par-smín-pa ṅés-par myóń-ste (sdig pa byas na rnam par smin pa nges par myong ste) as retribution for a sin committed is sure to take place, will certainly follow Dzl.; dé-ḍra-ba źig ṅés-par yód-na (de 'dra ba zhig nges par yod na) if such a one is really present Dzl.; ṅés-par ₒčí-ba (nges par 'chi ba) the certain dying, the certainty of death Thgy.; bdag ṅés-par byao (bdag nges par bya'o) I will surely do it Dzl. 2. by all means, to add force to the imperative mood Tar. 16, 11. — 2. often it is used subjectively, esp. in C., when séms-la (sems la) is to be supplied, so that it may be rendered by to know: bdén-par ṅes (bden par nges), rdzún-par ṅes (rdzun par nges) I know (I am certain) that it is true, untrue; ṅés-pa čer med (nges pa cher med) I am not quite sure, I do not know for certain, I do not fully understand, I do not clearly see through it Mil.; séms-čan ₒdi bdág-gi pa-má yin ṅés-na (sems can 'di bdag gi pha ma yin nges na) if we take it for granted, if we try to realize the fact, that this being is our father or mother Thgy.; to remember, to bear in mind *sém-la ṅe túb-bam* (sems la nge thub bam) C. shall you be able to remember that? ṅés-dón (nges don), also yáń-dag-doń (yang dag don), is said to mean immediate knowledge of the truth, which may be obtained mystically by continued contemplation, and is opp. to dráń-don (drang don), knowledge obtainable through the medium of the sacred writings Mil., also Lexx.; ṅes(-par) ₒbyuń-(ba) (nges par 'byung ba) Mil. frq., Schr.: 'deliverance from the round of transmigration', Sch.: 'to appear, to prove true'; another explanation still: 'knowledge of one's self' is not borne out by etymology. — ṅes-bzúń (nges bzung) acc. to Lexx. a synonym of brnán-pa (brnan pa), q. v. — ṅes-(par) légs(-pa) (nges par legs pa) Thgy., 'that which evidently is the best', is said to denote deliverance from the round of transmigration.

1. resp. źal-ṅó (zhal ngo) (cf. ṅó-bo (ngo bo), ṅor (ngor), ṅos (ngos)) face, countenance, air, look, as the expression of a man's personality and mind (ṅo mdzés-pa (ngo mdzes pa) Cs., and ṅán-pa (ngan pa) Schr. are dubious), bdág-gi ṅo-la gzigs-nas (bdag gi ngo la gzigs nas) when she (my mother) shall see my face, ṅo dkár-po (ngo dkar po) a cheerful face; ṅo nág-par ₒdug-pa (ngo nag par 'dug pa) to sit
